name: Korean SaaS product review
summary: Reviews Korean B2B software for password-manager support, optional consent, operator density, Korean wrapping, and accessible repeated work.

criteria.title: Declare enterprise password autofill intent
criteria.question: Can password managers distinguish sign-in credentials from password creation?
criteria.evidence: Inspect password inputs for current-password or new-password autocomplete tokens.
verification: Exercise sign-in, invited-user setup, and password reset with a platform password manager.
falsePositiveNotes: SSO-only screens without password inputs are outside this rule.

criteria.title: Keep product marketing consent optional
criteria.question: Is promotional consent separate from account and service terms and off by default?
criteria.evidence: Inspect checked checkboxes whose associated label mentions marketing, advertising, or promotional messages.
verification: Create a fresh account and confirm optional promotional consent is unchecked and independently operable.
falsePositiveNotes: Do not treat an administrator's existing organization policy summary as a new user opt-in.

criteria.title: Fit Korean operator density to the task
criteria.question: Do tables, forms, trees, and filters support efficient repeated work without shrinking text, focus, or targets below the accessibility floor?
criteria.evidence: Capture primary dashboard, table, form, and settings workflows at both declared viewports and every supported density setting.
verification: Check row count per fold, tabular numerals, sticky headers, focus, keyboard order, overflow, and persisted density preference.
falsePositiveNotes: Korean B2B products may intentionally use cozy or compact density when hit areas and legibility remain intact.

criteria.title: Let Korean operational copy wrap without breaking controls
criteria.question: Do long Korean labels, validation messages, table headers, and role names wrap or truncate with a recoverable full-text path?
criteria.evidence: Capture longest production-like Korean strings at mobile and desktop widths with zoom and text scaling applied.
verification: Check 200% zoom, Korean IME composition, dynamic validation, table headers, buttons, and permission names.
falsePositiveNotes: Stable identifiers and compact numeric columns may remain single-line when full text is available elsewhere.

criteria.title: Prove repeated operator workflows with assistive technology
criteria.question: Can keyboard and screen-reader users filter, edit, save, recover, and repeat core work without losing state or focus?
criteria.evidence: Attach keyboard order, accessibility-tree, state announcement, contrast, and repeated-action evidence.
verification: Run the approved browser verification profile at mobile and desktop viewports.
falsePositiveNotes: A passing component snapshot does not prove table navigation, focus restoration, or async save feedback.
The fixture intentionally omits password autocomplete and preselects marketing consent; compliant forms must not emit those confirmed findings.

notes: The pack reviews supplied evidence only. It does not read tenant data, change settings, or execute operator actions.
